title: Best European Destinations for 2026: Where to Go and Where to Stay
excerpt: From hidden gems in Portugal to classic favorites like Paris and Rome, discover the top European cities to visit this year and where to find the best hotel deals.
content:
Europe continues to be one of the most popular travel regions in the world, offering an incredible diversity of cultures, landscapes, cuisines, and experiences. Whether you are a first-time visitor or a seasoned European traveler, 2026 brings exciting opportunities to explore both classic favorites and emerging destinations.

## Paris, France

The City of Light remains an essential European destination. Hotel prices are competitive, with excellent deals available in the spring and fall shoulder seasons. The culinary scene continues to evolve, making Paris a feast for all senses. Expect to pay $120-350 per night for well-located hotels.

## Lisbon, Portugal

Lisbon has emerged as one of Europe's hottest destinations, and for good reason. The city offers stunning architecture, world-class seafood, vibrant nightlife, and some of the most affordable hotel prices in Western Europe. The nearby Sintra region adds a magical day-trip option. Hotels start from just $60 per night.

## Barcelona, Spain

Barcelona combines beach culture with world-class architecture, art, and cuisine. Gaudi's masterpieces, the Gothic Quarter, and the beachfront promenade create a city that appeals to every type of traveler. Hotel deals are particularly attractive from October through March, with rates starting at $80 per night.

## Rome, Italy

The Eternal City offers 2,800 years of history at every turn. From the Colosseum to Vatican City, Roman cuisine to gelato on every corner, Rome delivers an unforgettable experience. Budget-conscious travelers will find excellent value in neighborhoods like Trastevere and Testaccio, from $90 per night.

## Dubrovnik, Croatia

Known as the Pearl of the Adriatic, Dubrovnik offers stunning medieval architecture, crystal-clear waters, and Croatian hospitality. Visiting in May or September-October provides perfect weather with fewer tourists and lower hotel rates starting at $70 per night.

## Amsterdam, Netherlands

Amsterdam's canals, museums, and cycling culture make it a perennial favorite. The Van Gogh Museum, Anne Frank House, and vibrant food scene draw millions. Hotels in the canal district start around $130 per night, with better deals in the Jordaan and De Pijp neighborhoods.

## Planning Your European Trip

The key to a great European vacation is planning ahead. Use price comparison tools to find the best hotel deals, consider traveling during shoulder season for the best combination of weather and prices, and don't overlook smaller cities and towns that often offer the most authentic experiences at a fraction of the cost.
